(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) IN ADDITION, THE MOVING PARTY MAY BRING ANOTHER MOTION WITH THE PROPER SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS AFTER DISCOVERY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
E. Gerard Mannion, Esq. 415-733-1050 of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler for Plaintiff.
Randy Moss, Esq. 415-949-1900 of Demler Armstrong & Rowland for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative summary adjudication is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production.

Plaintiff's complaint alleges that defendant "waived and/or are estopped and/or have forfeited any right to deny coverage." (Complaint, 11.) "[W]here an insurer reserves its right to claim non coverage under the policy, notice of the reservation must be given to the insured or the reservation is deemed waived." (Miller v. Elite Insurance Co. (1980) 100 Cal.App.3d 739, 753-754.)

(see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kelly Mannion, Esq. 415-733-1050 of Mannion Lowe for Plaintiff.
Randy Moss, Esq. 415-949-1900 of Demler Armstrong & Rowland LLP for Defendant FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S Motion For Change Of Venue.

Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for change of venue is DENIED without Prejudice. There is a presumption in favor of the plaintiff's choice of forum. (Bechtel Corp. v. Superior Court (1973) 33 Cal.App.3d 405, 407, n.1.) Defendant does not contest that venue is proper is San Francisco County and it has failed to meet its burden under CCP section 397(c) that changing the venue would be in the interests of justice or for the convenience of the witnesses. (Smith v. Stanford Research Institute (1963) 212 Cal.App.2d 750, 754 ("[A] corporate defendant seeking a change of venue has the burden of negating the propriety of venue laid on all possible grounds.").) A party moving for change of venue is required to use supporting declarations setting forth witnesses, the nature of the testimony, and the reasons why they would be inconvenienced by the current forum. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) Defendant has produced no declarations regarding what witnesses it plans to use and how those witnesses would be inconvenienced by the case continuing in San Francisco. By contrast, Plaintiff has produced a declaration from its chief witness, Richard Reynolds, explaining the importance of his testimony in the action and why he would be inconvenienced by a change of venue.

In addition, the Moving party may bring another motion with the proper supporting documents after Discovery. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

There being no appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for change of venue is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Defendant's motion to change venue is based on the convenience of witnesses. (Memorandum in Support of Motion at page 2: lines 15-17.) "The court will not entertain a motion for change of venue on the ground of convenience of witnesses when the defendant has not filed an answer, for the reason that until the issues are joined the court cannot determine what testimony will be material. For the same reason, a motion for change of venue cannot be defeated on the ground of convenience of witnesses until an answer is filed." Cholakian & Associates v. Superior Court (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 361, 368. Defendant may refile the motion after it has served an answer to the complaint. Order signed in open court. (see order for full details)

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Rebecca Tovar, CSR 14200, 415.551.3778, Email: RTovar@sftc.org. =(302/ACM)
